grey-go-away-bird.size: Medium-sized turaco; similar to a large pigeon
grey-go-away-bird.habitat: Arid to moist open woodlands and thorn savanna, especially near surface water
grey-go-away-bird.diet: Forages in treetops; feeds on fruits, berries, and plant matter
grey-go-away-bird.migration: Largely resident within the southern Afrotropics

hadada-ibis.size: Medium-sized ibis with stout legs and a down-curved bill
hadada-ibis.habitat: Wetlands, cultivated land, and gardens; often near human habitation
hadada-ibis.diet: Probes soil and shallow water for invertebrates, insects, and worms
hadada-ibis.migration: Non-migratory; makes nomadic movements in response to rainfall during droughts

egyptian-goose.size: Large waterfowl, typical of medium-large geese
egyptian-goose.habitat: Wetlands, rivers, lakes, and open grassland across Africa; also ornamental parks worldwide
egyptian-goose.diet: Grazes on grasses, seeds, leaves, and grain
egyptian-goose.migration: Largely resident; introduced populations also non-migratory

spur-winged-goose.size: Large Sub-Saharan African waterbird; one of the largest waterfowl
spur-winged-goose.habitat: Wetlands, rivers, floodplains, and lakes in sub-Saharan Africa
spur-winged-goose.diet: Grazes on aquatic vegetation, seeds, and small invertebrates
spur-winged-goose.migration: Largely resident; may make local movements following water availability

african-jacana.size: Small-to-medium wader with exceptionally long toes and claws
african-jacana.habitat: Shallow lakes and wetlands with floating vegetation across sub-Saharan Africa
african-jacana.diet: Picks insects, small invertebrates, and seeds from floating vegetation
african-jacana.migration: Largely resident and sedentary within sub-Saharan Africa

red-billed-hornbill.habitat: Savannas and woodlands of sub-Saharan Africa
red-billed-hornbill.diet: Omnivorous; feeds on insects, small vertebrates, seeds, and fruit
red-billed-hornbill.migration: Largely resident within its savanna range

southern-ground-hornbill.size: Largest hornbill species in the world; crow-to-turkey sized ground bird
southern-ground-hornbill.habitat: Woodlands and savannas from Kenya to South Africa
southern-ground-hornbill.diet: Carnivorous; hunts insects, small vertebrates including snakes and lizards on the ground

southern-yellow-billed-hornbill.size: Small-to-medium hornbill; similar in size to other Tockus hornbills
southern-yellow-billed-hornbill.habitat: Dry thornveld and broad-leafed woodlands; often seen along roads and watercourses
southern-yellow-billed-hornbill.diet: Forages on the ground for seeds, small insects, spiders, and scorpions
southern-yellow-billed-hornbill.migration: Resident and widespread throughout southern Africa

helmeted-guineafowl.size: Large, rounded gamebird; comparable to a small turkey
helmeted-guineafowl.habitat: Open savanna, grassland, and woodland across sub-Saharan Africa
helmeted-guineafowl.diet: Omnivorous; seeds, tubers, insects, and small invertebrates

vulturine-guineafowl.size: Largest extant guinea fowl species; distinctly large and long-necked
vulturine-guineafowl.habitat: Arid and semi-arid bush and savanna in northeast Africa
vulturine-guineafowl.diet: Seeds, berries, insects, and plant matter foraged from the ground
vulturine-guineafowl.migration: Resident breeder in northeast Africa; non-migratory

ostrich.size: Largest and heaviest living bird; adults weigh 63.5–145 kg
ostrich.habitat: Open savanna, grassland, and semi-arid plains of sub-Saharan Africa
ostrich.diet: Omnivorous; mainly plants, seeds, roots, and occasionally insects and small vertebrates
ostrich.migration: Largely resident; some nomadic movements following food and water

kori-bustard.size: Largest flying bird native to Africa; males may be the heaviest flying animal
kori-bustard.habitat: Open grasslands, savanna, and lightly wooded areas across Africa
kori-bustard.diet: Opportunistic omnivore; insects, lizards, small mammals, seeds, and carrion
kori-bustard.migration: Largely resident; may make local seasonal movements

cape-weaver.habitat: Fynbos, open woodland, gardens, and wetland margins in southern Africa

southern-masked-weaver.habitat: Very wide range including shrubland, savanna, grassland, wetlands, semi-desert, and suburban gardens
southern-masked-weaver.migration: Resident and non-migratory throughout southern Africa

red-billed-quelea.size: Approximately 12 cm long; weighs 15–26 g; sparrow-sized
red-billed-quelea.habitat: Open savanna, grassland, and agricultural land across sub-Saharan Africa; avoids forests, deserts, and high altitudes
red-billed-quelea.diet: Primarily seeds of annual grasses; also causes major damage to cereal crops
red-billed-quelea.migration: Seasonally nomadic; moves to areas of recent rainfall and plentiful grass seed

village-weaver.habitat: Open and semi-open habitats including woodlands, towns, villages, and hotel grounds across sub-Saharan Africa
village-weaver.diet: Principally seeds and grain; also insects, especially when feeding young

long-tailed-widowbird.size: Medium-sized bird; breeding males have tail feathers approximately half a metre (20 inches) long
long-tailed-widowbird.habitat: Grasslands and open habitats in southern and central Africa
long-tailed-widowbird.migration: Largely resident within its range in southern and central Africa

cape-sugarbird.size: Slender bird with a long tail; one of the larger sugarbirds
cape-sugarbird.habitat: Fynbos biome of the Western Cape and Eastern Cape, South Africa
cape-sugarbird.diet: Primarily nectar from Protea flowers; also insects
cape-sugarbird.migration: Largely resident; endemic to the Fynbos biome

speckled-mousebird.size: Largest mousebird species; small but with a long tail, sparrow-sized body
speckled-mousebird.habitat: Forests, woodlands, gardens, and scrub across Central, Eastern, and Southern Africa
speckled-mousebird.diet: Fruit, berries, buds, and plant matter; occasionally insects

african-grey-parrot.size: Medium-sized parrot; one of the largest African parrots
african-grey-parrot.habitat: Dense forests and forest edges of west and central Africa
african-grey-parrot.diet: Seeds, nuts, fruits, and berries; occasional leafy vegetation
african-grey-parrot.migration: Largely resident; may make local movements for food

senegal-parrot.size: Small-to-medium parrot typical of the Poicephalus genus
senegal-parrot.habitat: Open woodland, savanna, and farms across west Africa
senegal-parrot.diet: Fruit, seeds, blossoms, and grain; considered a crop pest on maize and millet
senegal-parrot.migration: Makes seasonal migrations within west Africa following fruit availability

fischers-lovebird.size: Small parrot; typical lovebird size, roughly 15 cm long
fischers-lovebird.habitat: Savanna and open woodland in East Africa, particularly Tanzania

rosy-faced-lovebird.habitat: Arid savanna woodlands of Namibia and adjacent areas
rosy-faced-lovebird.diet: Seeds, berries, and plant matter; eats throughout the day
rosy-faced-lovebird.migration: Largely resident; non-migratory within Namibian savanna range

crowned-lapwing.habitat: Open grassland, dry savanna, and lightly grazed areas from Somalia to southern Africa
crowned-lapwing.diet: Insects, worms, and other small invertebrates foraged from the ground
crowned-lapwing.migration: Largely resident; non-migratory within its range

blacksmith-lapwing.habitat: Wetland margins, flooded grasslands, and open areas near water from Kenya to southern Africa
blacksmith-lapwing.migration: Largely resident; non-migratory within its range

african-penguin.habitat: Coastal waters, rocky shores, and islands of southern Africa (South Africa and Namibia)
african-penguin.diet: Primarily fish and squid; a pursuit diver
african-penguin.migration: Largely resident along southern African coasts; non-migratory

cape-gannet.size: Large seabird; typical gannet proportions with a pointed bill
cape-gannet.habitat: Open ocean and coastal waters off southern Africa; breeds in large colonies on islands
cape-gannet.diet: Fish; plunge-dives at speeds of 40–86 km/h to catch prey
cape-gannet.migration: Disperses along southern African coasts after breeding; partially migratory

goliath-heron.size: Very large heron; the world's largest heron species
goliath-heron.habitat: Large rivers, lakes, and coastal wetlands in sub-Saharan Africa and parts of South Asia
goliath-heron.diet: Primarily large fish; also frogs and other aquatic prey
goliath-heron.migration: Largely resident; non-migratory within its range

verreauxs-eagle.size: Large eagle; one of the largest African raptors with a broad wingspan
verreauxs-eagle.habitat: Hilly and mountainous regions with rocky outcrops in southern and eastern Africa
verreauxs-eagle.diet: Highly specialized on rock hyraxes; also takes small antelopes, hares, gamebirds, and monkeys when hyrax numbers are low
verreauxs-eagle.migration: Largely resident; non-migratory within its mountain range

pels-fishing-owl.size: Large owl species; one of the largest African owls
pels-fishing-owl.habitat: Dense riparian forest along slow-moving rivers and lake shores in sub-Saharan Africa
pels-fishing-owl.diet: Fish and frogs snatched nocturnally from the surface of rivers and lakes
pels-fishing-owl.migration: Largely resident along suitable river systems

pin-tailed-whydah.size: Small songbird; breeding males have a conspicuous pennant-like tail
pin-tailed-whydah.habitat: Open grassland, savanna, and gardens across sub-Saharan Africa

red-junglefowl.size: Medium-sized gamebird; ancestor of the domestic chicken
red-junglefowl.habitat: Tropical forests, forest edges, and scrub across Southeast Asia and parts of South Asia
red-junglefowl.diet: Omnivorous; seeds, grain, insects, and small invertebrates

indian-pond-heron.size: Small heron; compact and stocky build
indian-pond-heron.habitat: Edges of small water-bodies, paddyfields, wetlands, and areas near human habitation in South and Southeast Asia
indian-pond-heron.diet: Fish, frogs, insects, and other small prey stalked at water's edge
indian-pond-heron.migration: Largely resident; some populations make short seasonal movements

painted-stork.size: Large wading stork; typical of the Mycteria genus
painted-stork.habitat: Wetlands, shallow rivers, and lakes of tropical Asia south of the Himalayas
painted-stork.diet: Primarily small fish sensed by touch; sweeps half-open bill through shallow water
painted-stork.migration: Not migratory; makes short-distance movements in response to weather, food, or breeding

asian-openbill.size: Large wading bird; typical stork proportions
asian-openbill.habitat: Wetlands, floodplains, and rice paddies across the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia
asian-openbill.diet: Primarily snails; the gap in the bill is an adaptation for handling them
asian-openbill.migration: Resident within range; makes long-distance movements in response to weather and food availability

indian-roller.size: 30–34 cm long; wingspan 65–74 cm; weight 166–176 g
indian-roller.habitat: Open grassland, scrub forest, and agricultural areas from West Asia to the Indian subcontinent
indian-roller.diet: Mainly insects, especially beetles; caught from a perch on roadside trees or wires

white-throated-kingfisher.size: Medium-sized tree kingfisher; larger than common kingfisher
white-throated-kingfisher.habitat: Widespread across Asia from the Sinai to Indonesia; found well away from water in forests, farmland, and urban areas
white-throated-kingfisher.diet: Wide range of prey including small reptiles, amphibians, crabs, small rodents, and birds
white-throated-kingfisher.migration: Mostly resident; some populations make short-distance movements

asian-green-bee-eater.size: Small bee-eater; slender with elongated tail feathers
asian-green-bee-eater.habitat: Grassland, thin scrub, and open forest from southern Iran to Vietnam; often far from water
asian-green-bee-eater.diet: Mainly insects, especially bees and other flying insects caught in aerial sallies
asian-green-bee-eater.migration: Resident or makes short-distance seasonal movements within its Asian range

oriental-pied-hornbill.size: Among the smallest Asian hornbills; medium-large canopy bird
oriental-pied-hornbill.habitat: Subtropical and tropical moist lowland forests across the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia
oriental-pied-hornbill.diet: Fruit, insects, shellfish, small reptiles, small mammals, and birds' eggs
oriental-pied-hornbill.migration: Largely resident within its forest range

rhinoceros-hornbill.size: Large forest hornbill; one of the biggest hornbill species
rhinoceros-hornbill.habitat: Lowland and montane tropical and subtropical rainforest up to 1,400 m in Borneo, Sumatra, Java, and the Malay Peninsula
rhinoceros-hornbill.diet: Fruit, insects, and small animals typical of large hornbills
rhinoceros-hornbill.migration: Largely resident within its forest range

indian-grey-hornbill.size: Medium-sized hornbill; one of the few urban hornbill species
indian-grey-hornbill.habitat: Indian subcontinent; urban areas with large avenue trees as well as forests
indian-grey-hornbill.diet: Fruit, berries, insects, and small vertebrates
indian-grey-hornbill.migration: Largely resident and non-migratory on the Indian subcontinent

coppersmith-barbet.size: Small barbet; compact and stocky, sparrow-sized
coppersmith-barbet.habitat: Gardens, orchards, and open woodland in the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia
coppersmith-barbet.diet: Predominantly frugivorous; also eats insects, especially winged termites
coppersmith-barbet.migration: Resident and non-migratory throughout its range

rose-ringed-parakeet.size: Medium-sized parrot; slender with a long tail
rose-ringed-parakeet.habitat: Tropical woodland and urban environments; native to Africa and the Indian subcontinent, now established in cities worldwide
rose-ringed-parakeet.diet: Seeds, fruits, berries, nuts, and grain; considered a crop pest
rose-ringed-parakeet.migration: Largely resident; established urban populations are non-migratory

alexandrine-parakeet.size: Medium-sized parrot; one of the largest Psittacula parakeets
alexandrine-parakeet.habitat: Forests, woodland, and mangroves across South Asia and Southeast Asia
alexandrine-parakeet.diet: Seeds, fruits, berries, blossoms, and grain
alexandrine-parakeet.migration: Largely resident; some populations make local seasonal movements

plum-headed-parakeet.size: Medium-sized parakeet; slender with a long tail
plum-headed-parakeet.habitat: Forest, woodland, and foothills across the Indian subcontinent
plum-headed-parakeet.diet: Seeds, grain, fruits, and berries; may raid crops
plum-headed-parakeet.migration: Largely resident; endemic to the Indian subcontinent

red-whiskered-bulbul.size: Small passerine; sparrow-sized with a distinctive pointed crest
red-whiskered-bulbul.habitat: Hill forests, urban gardens, and wooded areas across tropical Asia
red-whiskered-bulbul.diet: Fruits and small insects; primarily frugivorous
red-whiskered-bulbul.migration: Largely resident throughout its tropical Asian range

red-vented-bulbul.habitat: Open woodland, scrub, gardens, and agricultural areas across South Asia
red-vented-bulbul.migration: Largely resident; non-migratory across South Asia

oriental-magpie-robin.size: Small passerine; robin-sized with a long upright tail
oriental-magpie-robin.habitat: Urban gardens, open forests, and woodland across the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia
oriental-magpie-robin.diet: Insects and other small invertebrates; forages on the ground
oriental-magpie-robin.migration: Largely resident; non-migratory throughout its range

common-myna.habitat: Open woodland, urban environments, and agricultural areas; highly adaptable across Asia and introduced regions worldwide
common-myna.diet: Omnivorous; insects, fruit, grain, scraps, and small vertebrates
common-myna.migration: Largely resident; non-migratory but highly expansive in range

hill-myna.size: Medium-to-large myna; larger than common myna
hill-myna.habitat: Tropical forests and forest edges across southern Asia
hill-myna.migration: Largely resident in southern Asian forests

asian-koel.size: Medium-sized cuckoo; similar in size to a large dove
asian-koel.habitat: Forests, urban gardens, and cultivated areas across the Indian subcontinent, China, and Southeast Asia
asian-koel.diet: Largely frugivorous as adults; also insects
asian-koel.migration: Partially migratory; some populations move seasonally within the region

greater-coucal.size: Large non-parasitic cuckoo; crow-sized with a long tail
greater-coucal.habitat: Jungle, cultivation, and urban gardens across the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia
greater-coucal.diet: Insects, eggs, and nestlings of other birds; forages on the ground and in vegetation
greater-coucal.migration: Largely resident; non-migratory throughout its widespread range

house-crow.size: Approximately 40 cm long; slimmer than a carrion crow
house-crow.habitat: Urban and suburban areas, ports, and coastal towns across Asia; spread globally via shipping routes
house-crow.diet: Omnivorous scavenger; food scraps, insects, small vertebrates, and carrion
house-crow.migration: Largely resident; spread through assisted dispersal rather than migration

large-billed-crow.size: Large crow; similar in size to a common raven
large-billed-crow.habitat: Forests, urban areas, and a wide range of habitats across Asia
large-billed-crow.diet: Highly adaptable omnivore; eats almost anything including carrion, fruit, insects, and scraps
large-billed-crow.migration: Largely resident; non-migratory but highly adaptable and expansive

rufous-treepie.size: Large corvid with a long tail; magpie-sized
rufous-treepie.habitat: Open scrub, agricultural areas, forests, and urban gardens across the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia
rufous-treepie.diet: Omnivorous; insects, small vertebrates, eggs, fruit, and scraps

black-drongo.size: 28 cm long; small passerine with a distinctive forked tail
black-drongo.habitat: Open agricultural areas and light forest across tropical southern Asia from Iran to Indonesia
black-drongo.diet: Primarily insects caught in aerial sallies from an exposed perch
black-drongo.migration: Largely resident; some populations make short seasonal movements

greater-racket-tailed-drongo.size: Medium-sized drongo with elongated outer tail feathers tipped with webbing
greater-racket-tailed-drongo.habitat: Forest habitats across South and Southeast Asia
greater-racket-tailed-drongo.diet: Insects caught by aerial sallying; also steals prey from other foragers in mixed flocks
greater-racket-tailed-drongo.migration: Largely resident within its forest range

indian-paradise-flycatcher.size: 20–50 cm total length including elongated tail; males considerably longer due to central tail feathers
indian-paradise-flycatcher.habitat: Dense forest and wooded areas across the Indian subcontinent, Central Asia, and Southeast Asia
indian-paradise-flycatcher.diet: Insects including grasshoppers, butterflies, mantises, and flies; caught in the air
indian-paradise-flycatcher.migration: Partially migratory; resident populations in Sri Lanka and India, migratory in northern parts of range

purple-sunbird.size: Small sunbird; compact with a curved bill
purple-sunbird.habitat: Open woodland, gardens, and scrub across parts of the Arabian Peninsula and South to Southeast Asia
purple-sunbird.diet: Mainly nectar; also insects, especially when feeding young
purple-sunbird.migration: Largely resident; non-migratory within its range

crimson-sunbird.size: Small sunbird; typical compact sunbird proportions
crimson-sunbird.habitat: Forest, forest edges, and gardens across South and Southeast Asia
crimson-sunbird.diet: Largely nectar; also insects, especially when feeding young
